description: Returns a property of the document object, such as document.title or document.location.href. Use for page URL, title, referrer, protocol, pathname, search, hash, or domain. The variable always has a value; type depends on the property (the common properties listed are strings).

when_to_use: Get page URL, path, or query string for tracking or conditions
Read document title for page-type or content categorization
Use referrer for attribution or entry-point logic
Trigger tags based on which element has focus (activeElement)
common_use_cases: Page URL: location.href, URL, documentURI for full or partial URL
Page title: title for content or section identification
Referrer: referrer for traffic source or campaign logic
URL parts: location.pathname, location.search, location.hash, location.protocol, location.host
Focus: activeElement to trigger tags based on focused element
best_practices: Use the exact document property names (e.g. title, location.href, referrer)
Common properties are strings; document always provides a value
Use activeElement when you need to react to which element has focus
Prefer standard names (title, location.pathname) over aliases where both exist

create_variable.description: Create a document variable that returns the specified document property

value.description: Document property to read (API: value). E.g. title, location.href, location.pathname, referrer.
Document property to read (API: value). The variable returns that property's value at runtime.
value.recommendation: Use standard document property names. Common ones: title, location.protocol, location.host, location.href, location.pathname, location.search, location.hash, domain, URL, documentURI, referrer. Use activeElement for the element that currently has focus.

create_example.description: Create a document variable for page title

update_example.description: Update the document property for an existing document variable

common_mistakes: Using a non-existent document property – stick to supported names (title, location.*, referrer, etc.)
Expecting a different type – common document properties return strings
Confusing location.hash with URL fragment only – value is the fragment including # or empty

variable_empty_or_unexpected.problem: Document variable returns empty or unexpected value
solutions: Confirm the property name is correct (e.g. location.href not location.hrefs)
Check that the page has loaded when the variable is evaluated
For activeElement, ensure focus is on a document element when the variable runs
